raw_text:
JSON
{
  "phase_17": [
    {
      "id": "bd-17a0",
      "title": "Projection spine: cursor + idempotent apply contract",
      "description": "Introduce a shared projection contract that enforces (1) per-projection cursor persistence, (2) idempotent apply semantics, and (3) deterministic rebuild capability. Add a projection_cursor table (or equivalent) keyed by (projection_name, tenant_id/app_id) with last_event_id/stream_position and updated_at. Provide a library helper used by all projection workers.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a replay-governance engineer. Invariant: every projection advances its cursor monotonically and never applies the same event twice. Failure mode to avoid: drift between event stream and read model due to duplicate apply or missing cursor updates.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "A projection cursor persistence mechanism exists and is used by projection workers",
        "Cursor updates are transactional with read-model writes",
        "Projection apply API requires event_id/stream_position and returns whether it applied"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_cursor_contract_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Projection cursor contract test passes against real Postgres + NATS"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/projections/src/lib.rs",
        "platform/projections/src/cursor.rs",
        "platform/projections/db/migrations/*",
        "e2e-tests/tests/projection_cursor_contract_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17a1",
      "title": "Projection lag metrics and backlog gauges",
      "description": "Emit operational metrics for projection freshness: projection_lag_ms, projection_backlog_count, projection_last_applied_age_seconds. Metrics must be derived from cursor timestamps and stream position. Expose via existing /metrics endpoints.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like an SRE for evented systems. Invariant: projection freshness is observable and comparable across modules. Failure mode to avoid: silent staleness where the system is 'up' but serving stale reads.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "projection_lag_ms and projection_backlog_count appear in /metrics",
        "Metrics are labeled by projection_name and tenant/app context",
        "A failing projection visibly increases lag/backlog"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_lag_metrics_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Metrics test passes; lag/backlog are emitted under controlled load"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/projections/src/metrics.rs",
        "modules/ar/src/metrics.rs",
        "modules/payments/src/metrics.rs",
        "modules/subscriptions/src/metrics.rs",
        "modules/gl/src/metrics.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/projection_lag_metrics_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17a2",
      "title": "Deterministic projection rebuild tool (blue/green swap)",
      "description": "Create a rebuild tool that can: (1) build projections into a shadow schema/table set, (2) verify cursor and digest, (3) atomically swap readers to the rebuilt projection (blue/green). Must be deterministic given the same event stream and seed data.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a data-plane migration specialist. Invariant: a rebuild never serves partial state and swap is atomic. Failure mode to avoid: serving half-rebuilt projections or corrupting reads during rebuild.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Rebuild tool supports shadow build + atomic swap",
        "Swap is one transaction / one step from reader perspective",
        "Rebuild emits a digest that is stable across two runs on same stream"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_rebuild_blue_green_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Blue/green rebuild E2E passes with real Postgres + NATS"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 5,
      "files_involved": [
        "tools/projection-rebuild/src/main.rs",
        "tools/projection-rebuild/src/swap.rs",
        "platform/projections/src/rebuild.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/projection_rebuild_blue_green_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17a3",
      "title": "Fallback policy: HTTP fallback budgets + circuit breaker",
      "description": "Implement explicit HTTP fallback behavior for reads when projections are stale/unavailable. Enforce a time budget (e.g., 200ms) and a circuit breaker to prevent cascading failure. Record fallback_invocation_count and fallback_latency_ms.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a resilience engineer. Invariant: fallback does not overload write services and is bounded by time/circuit rules. Failure mode to avoid: projection outage causing system-wide thundering herd to HTTP fallback.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a1"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Fallback only activates when projection is stale beyond threshold",
        "Circuit breaker trips under sustained failures and prevents repeated fallbacks",
        "Metrics emitted for fallback count and latency"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_fallback_circuit_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Fallback E2E proves budget + breaker behavior with real services"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/projections/src/fallback.rs",
        "modules/ar/src/routes/*.rs",
        "modules/payments/src/routes/*.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/projection_fallback_circuit_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17a4",
      "title": "Replay certification: rebuild -> digest equality suite",
      "description": "Add certification tests that rebuild at least two core projections (e.g., AR invoice summary, Payments attempt summary) twice from the same event stream and asserts identical digests, cursor end state, and row counts. Must run integrated against real Postgres + NATS.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a deterministic systems verifier. Invariant: replay produces the same read-model state, not just 'no crashes'. Failure mode to avoid: hidden nondeterminism in projection application order or time-derived fields.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a2"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Two rebuild runs on identical stream produce identical digests",
        "Cursor end positions match across runs",
        "Digest computation is stable and versioned"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests replay_certification_digest_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Replay certification suite passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "e2e-tests/tests/replay_certification_digest_e2e.rs",
        "platform/projections/src/digest.rs",
        "tools/projection-rebuild/src/main.rs"
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "bd-17b0",
      "title": "Audit spine: append-only audit log schema + writer API",
      "description": "Create an append-only audit log (per module DB or centralized audit DB, consistent with your module boundaries). Define audit_event schema: audit_id, occurred_at, actor_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, before_hash/after_hash or before/after snapshots, causation_id/correlation_id/trace_id, mutation_class. Provide a single writer API used by modules.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a compliance auditor. Invariant: every audited mutation produces an immutable audit record with actor + causation metadata. Failure mode to avoid: mutable audit records or missing actor/correlation fields that make audits useless.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Audit log table exists and is append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE allowed)",
        "A shared audit writer API exists and is usable from all modules",
        "Audit schema includes envelope metadata + mutation_class"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_append_only_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Append-only audit E2E passes and UPDATE/DELETE is rejected by DB"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/audit/src/lib.rs",
        "platform/audit/db/migrations/*",
        "e2e-tests/tests/audit_append_only_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17b1",
      "title": "Audit rules by mutation_class (policy enforcement)",
      "description": "Implement enforceable rules: strict_immutable => no updates; compensating_required => must emit reversal/supersession linkage; mutable_with_audit => must emit field-level diff audit. Wire policy checks into mutation boundaries (guard/mutation layer).",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a financial controls engineer. Invariant: mutation_class determines required audit behavior and is enforced in code and DB. Failure mode to avoid: 'best effort' auditing where critical mutations slip through without audit artifacts.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17b0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Policy checks exist for strict_immutable, compensating_required, mutable_with_audit",
        "Violations fail closed (mutation rejected)",
        "Policy is unit-tested and exercised in E2E"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_policy_enforcement_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Audit policy enforcement E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/audit/src/policy.rs",
        "modules/ar/src/**/*.rs",
        "modules/payments/src/**/*.rs",
        "modules/subscriptions/src/**/*.rs",
        "modules/gl/src/**/*.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/audit_policy_enforcement_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17b2",
      "title": "Field-level diff auditing for mutable_with_audit",
      "description": "Implement field-diff capture for mutable_with_audit entities: record changed fields (name, old, new) or hashed snapshots with diff metadata. Ensure diffs are deterministic (stable ordering) and do not include nondeterministic timestamps unless explicitly allowed.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like an audit trail engineer. Invariant: diffs are complete, deterministic, and tied to actor/correlation metadata. Failure mode to avoid: partial diffs or nondeterministic ordering that breaks replay and verification.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17b1"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Diff entries are generated for at least one mutable_with_audit entity",
        "Diff ordering is deterministic across runs",
        "Audit entries include actor + trace/correlation ids"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_field_diff_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Field diff E2E passes and diffs match expected stable output"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/audit/src/diff.rs",
        "modules/*/src/**/*.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/audit_field_diff_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17b3",
      "title": "Actor identity propagation into audit + envelope",
      "description": "Standardize actor identity (service actor, user actor) propagation from HTTP entrypoints and internal schedulers into envelope metadata and audit entries. Define actor types and enforce presence where required by policy.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a security/compliance engineer. Invariant: every audited mutation has a non-empty actor identity with a defined type. Failure mode to avoid: anonymous mutations that cannot be attributed.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17b0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Actor identity is present in audit entries for covered mutations",
        "Schedulers use a service actor identity",
        "E2E test validates actor fields are populated"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_actor_propagation_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Actor propagation E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/audit/src/actor.rs",
        "modules/*/src/routes/*.rs",
        "modules/*/src/scheduler/*.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/audit_actor_propagation_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17b4",
      "title": "Audit completeness oracle: mutation -> exactly one audit record",
      "description": "Extend the existing oracle spine to include audit completeness checks for a defined set of mutations: for each mutation event (by idempotency_key/event_id), assert exactly one audit record exists and links via causation/correlation/trace.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a verification engineer. Invariant: audited mutations produce exactly one audit artifact (no gaps, no doubles). Failure mode to avoid: duplicate audit records on replay or missing records on edge paths.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17b1"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Oracle includes audit completeness checks for defined mutation set",
        "E2E suite runs oracle after scenarios and validates audit completeness"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests -- --nocapture",
        "All E2E tests pass with audit completeness checks enabled"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "e2e-tests/tests/oracle.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/*_e2e.rs",
        "platform/audit/src/lib.rs"
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "bd-17c0",
      "title": "Tenant registry + provisioning schema baseline",
      "description": "Create a control-plane tenant registry (lightweight) that tracks tenant_id, status, created_at, module schema versions, and environment. Provide a deterministic provisioning sequence definition (steps and checks).",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a platform operations engineer. Invariant: tenant provisioning is repeatable and observable with a single source of truth. Failure mode to avoid: ad-hoc tenant setup that drifts and breaks upgrades.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Tenant registry schema exists and can store per-module schema versions",
        "Provisioning steps are defined and include verification checks"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests tenant_registry_smoke_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Tenant registry smoke E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/tenant-registry/src/lib.rs",
        "platform/tenant-registry/db/migrations/*",
        "e2e-tests/tests/tenant_registry_smoke_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17c1",
      "title": "Tenant provisioning CLI (create/activate/verify)",
      "description": "Build a CLI tool to provision tenants deterministically: create tenant record, initialize required module DBs, run migrations, seed minimal data, and run verification checks (health/version endpoints).",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a release engineer. Invariant: tenant creation produces a fully-migrated, verifiable tenant with no manual steps. Failure mode to avoid: partial provisioning that passes superficially but fails during billing cycles.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17c0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "CLI supports: tenant create, tenant activate, tenant verify",
        "Verify calls real /ready and /version endpoints for all modules",
        "Provisioning produces deterministic outcomes given same inputs"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo run -p tenantctl -- tenant create --tenant t1 && cargo run -p tenantctl -- tenant verify --tenant t1",
        "CLI completes successfully and verification passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "tools/tenantctl/src/main.rs",
        "tools/tenantctl/src/provision.rs",
        "tools/tenantctl/src/verify.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17c2",
      "title": "Multi-tenant migration runner (N tenants, deterministic)",
      "description": "Implement a migration runner that can apply migrations across N tenant databases deterministically with bounded concurrency, clear logging, and failure reporting. Must not require tenant_id threaded through app code; this is an ops tool.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a database fleet operator. Invariant: migrations across many tenant DBs are repeatable, ordered, and safe under concurrency. Failure mode to avoid: partial fleet migration with no visibility or inconsistent schema versions.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17c0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Runner can migrate N tenant DBs with bounded parallelism",
        "Runner records per-tenant schema version results into tenant registry",
        "Failures are resumable without reapplying completed tenants"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo run -p tenantctl -- fleet migrate --tenants 10 --parallel 4",
        "Fleet migration completes; registry reflects schema versions"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "tools/tenantctl/src/fleet_migrate.rs",
        "platform/tenant-registry/src/lib.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17c3",
      "title": "Tenant suspension/deprovision flow (safe + auditable)",
      "description": "Implement tenant lifecycle transitions: suspend (stop schedulers / block writes per policy), and deprovision (archive/retain per retention policy). Must emit audit records and update registry.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a compliance + ops engineer. Invariant: tenant lifecycle changes are reversible (where allowed) and auditable, and do not leak resources. Failure mode to avoid: suspended tenants still mutating ledgers or deprovision deleting data that must be retained.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17c1", "bd-17b0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Suspend blocks billing schedulers and write mutations per policy",
        "Deprovision follows retention policy and records actions in audit log",
        "Registry reflects lifecycle status accurately"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests tenant_lifecycle_suspend_deprovision_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Lifecycle E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 5,
      "files_involved": [
        "tools/tenantctl/src/lifecycle.rs",
        "platform/tenant-registry/src/lib.rs",
        "platform/audit/src/lib.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/tenant_lifecycle_suspend_deprovision_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-17c4",
      "title": "Scale simulation: 100 tenants billing digest stability",
      "description": "Extend deterministic simulation harness patterns to run 100 tenants through compressed cycles, then validate projection lag bounds, audit completeness, and digest stability. This is not performance tuning; it is correctness-under-scale validation.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a systems verification lead. Invariant: correctness properties (no duplicates, audit completeness, projection determinism) hold as tenant count scales. Failure mode to avoid: scale introduces race conditions and nondeterminism.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a4", "bd-17b4", "bd-17c2"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "100-tenant run completes with stable digests for key projections",
        "Audit completeness oracle passes for covered mutations",
        "Projection lag metrics remain within configured bounds during run"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests scale_100_tenants_truth_at_scale_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Scale E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 5,
      "files_involved": [
        "tools/simulation/src/main.rs",
        "tools/projection-rebuild/src/main.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/scale_100_tenants_truth_at_scale_e2e.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/oracle.rs"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "phase_18": [
    {
      "id": "bd-18a0",
      "title": "Release pipeline: promote artifacts across environments",
      "description": "Create a promotion pipeline that builds versioned module artifacts, publishes them to an internal registry (or GitHub releases if that is your baseline), and supports promotion dev -> staging -> prod with immutable artifact hashes. Must tie module version, schema version, and projection/audit versions together.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a release manager. Invariant: the exact artifact deployed is traceable and immutable across environments. Failure mode to avoid: environment drift and 'works in staging' due to rebuilding different binaries.",
      "depends_on": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "CI produces immutable artifacts with checksums",
        "Promotion uses existing artifacts (no rebuild) and records provenance",
        "Deploy metadata includes module version + schema version + projection version"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests release_provenance_smoke_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Release provenance E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        ".github/workflows/release.yml",
        ".github/workflows/promote.yml",
        "docs/ops/RELEASE-PROMOTION.md",
        "e2e-tests/tests/release_provenance_smoke_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-18a1",
      "title": "Secrets and config hardening (env contract + validation)",
      "description": "Define a strict configuration contract per module (required env vars, defaults, and validation). Add startup validation that fails fast with clear errors. Ensure tenant tools also validate required config.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like an operations hardening engineer. Invariant: services never start with partial/misconfigured secrets or unsafe defaults. Failure mode to avoid: running with missing keys leading to corrupt external integrations or insecure behavior.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-18a0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Each module validates required env vars on startup",
        "Validation errors are explicit and actionable",
        "E2E test proves a module refuses to start with missing required config"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests config_validation_failfast_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Config failfast E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 3,
      "files_involved": [
        "modules/ar/src/config.rs",
        "modules/payments/src/config.rs",
        "modules/subscriptions/src/config.rs",
        "modules/gl/src/config.rs",
        "modules/notifications/src/config.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/config_validation_failfast_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-18b0",
      "title": "Service-to-service auth (signed internal requests)",
      "description": "Introduce service-to-service authentication for internal HTTP fallbacks and ops endpoints (where applicable). Use signed tokens or mTLS (pick one consistent pattern). Ensure tenant tools authenticate when calling /ready, /version, fallback routes.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a platform security engineer. Invariant: internal endpoints are not anonymously callable and requests are verifiable. Failure mode to avoid: unauthorized internal calls triggering mutations or leaking sensitive status data.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a3", "bd-17c1"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Internal HTTP calls include verifiable auth",
        "Unauthorized requests are rejected with 401/403",
        "E2E test confirms authenticated success and unauthenticated failure"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests service_to_service_auth_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Service-to-service auth E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 5,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/security/src/lib.rs",
        "modules/*/src/middleware/auth.rs",
        "tools/tenantctl/src/verify.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/service_to_service_auth_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-18b1",
      "title": "RBAC for operator actions (tenant lifecycle + rebuild)",
      "description": "Add role-based authorization for operator actions: tenant suspend/deprovision, projection rebuild/swap, fleet migrate. Define roles (operator, admin, auditor) and enforce them in tools and any HTTP endpoints used.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a governance/security lead. Invariant: high-impact operational actions require explicit authorization. Failure mode to avoid: anyone with network access can suspend tenants or rebuild projections.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-18b0", "bd-17c3", "bd-17a2"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Roles are defined and enforced for operational actions",
        "Unauthorized role attempts are rejected and audited",
        "E2E test covers allowed/denied cases"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests rbac_operator_actions_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "RBAC E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/security/src/rbac.rs",
        "tools/tenantctl/src/lifecycle.rs",
        "tools/projection-rebuild/src/main.rs",
        "tools/tenantctl/src/fleet_migrate.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/rbac_operator_actions_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-18c0",
      "title": "Dashboards: SLO views for lag, UNKNOWN, audit failures",
      "description": "Create Grafana dashboards (or your chosen stack) for: projection lag/backlog, payment UNKNOWN aging, invariant failures, audit completeness errors, tenant provisioning status. Dashboards must match metrics produced in Phase 17/16.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like an SRE building operational truth. Invariant: operators can see leading indicators before customers call. Failure mode to avoid: having metrics but no actionable visualization and therefore no operational feedback loop.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a1", "bd-17b4", "bd-16d2"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Dashboard JSON/provisioning exists in repo",
        "Panels map to real metrics names and labels",
        "A smoke test (manual ok) confirms dashboards load with live data"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d",
        "Grafana loads dashboards and panels show non-empty data under test load"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 2,
      "files_involved": [
        "infra/monitoring/grafana/dashboards/projection-lag.json",
        "infra/monitoring/grafana/dashboards/billing-unknown.json",
        "infra/monitoring/grafana/dashboards/audit-integrity.json"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-18c1",
      "title": "DR drill: restore + replay rebuild + oracle pass",
      "description": "Automate a disaster recovery drill that: restores DB backups into scratch, replays events to rebuild projections, runs cross-module oracle + audit completeness checks, and outputs a digest report. This is a full-stack correctness drill.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a disaster recovery engineer. Invariant: you can restore and re-derive read models and still pass oracles. Failure mode to avoid: backups restore but system cannot reach correct state due to missing events or nondeterministic projections.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a4", "bd-17b4", "bd-16g1"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "DR drill script exists and is runnable end-to-end",
        "Oracle passes after restore + rebuild",
        "Produces a digest report artifact"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && bash tools/backup/dr_drill_restore_rebuild.sh",
        "Script completes and reports ORACLE PASS + stable digests"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 5,
      "files_involved": [
        "tools/backup/dr_drill_restore_rebuild.sh",
        "tools/projection-rebuild/src/main.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/oracle.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-18d0",
      "title": "Rate limiting and overload protection for read paths",
      "description": "Add rate limits for read-heavy endpoints and fallback paths, with tenant-aware quotas. Ensure overload does not cascade into write services. Emit reject metrics.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like a production traffic engineer. Invariant: overload is contained and does not degrade write correctness. Failure mode to avoid: read spikes causing fallback storms and starving core billing mutations.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17a3"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Tenant-aware rate limit exists for selected endpoints",
        "Fallback path has tighter limits than normal reads",
        "E2E test shows limits engage and system remains healthy"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests rate_limit_overload_protection_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Rate limiting E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "platform/security/src/ratelimit.rs",
        "modules/*/src/middleware/ratelimit.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/rate_limit_overload_protection_e2e.rs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "bd-18e0",
      "title": "Compliance export: audit + ledger extract per tenant",
      "description": "Provide a deterministic export tool to produce tenant-scoped extracts for audit logs and core ledgers (AR/Payments/GL) with stable ordering and checksums. Output format: CSV/JSON Lines with manifest and hashes.",
      "how_to_think": "Think like an external auditor integrator. Invariant: exports are tenant-scoped, complete, and tamper-evident. Failure mode to avoid: partial exports or nondeterministic ordering that prevents reconciliation.",
      "depends_on": ["bd-17b0", "bd-17c0"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        "Export tool generates manifests with checksums",
        "Exports are stable across two runs with same source state",
        "Includes audit log + at least AR invoices + payment attempts + GL entries"
      ],
      "verification_commands": [
        "docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests compliance_export_determinism_e2e -- --nocapture",
        "Compliance export E2E passes"
      ],
      "estimated_complexity": 4,
      "files_involved": [
        "tools/compliance-export/src/main.rs",
        "tools/compliance-export/src/export.rs",
        "e2e-tests/tests/compliance_export_determinism_e2e.rs"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

phase_17.title: Projection spine: cursor + idempotent apply contract
phase_17.description: Introduce a shared projection contract that enforces (1) per-projection cursor persistence, (2) idempotent apply semantics, and (3) deterministic rebuild capability. Add a projection_cursor table (or equivalent) keyed by (projection_name, tenant_id/app_id) with last_event_id/stream_position and updated_at. Provide a library helper used by all projection workers.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a replay-governance engineer. Invariant: every projection advances its cursor monotonically and never applies the same event twice. Failure mode to avoid: drift between event stream and read model due to duplicate apply or missing cursor updates.
acceptance_criteria: A projection cursor persistence mechanism exists and is used by projection workers
Cursor updates are transactional with read-model writes
Projection apply API requires event_id/stream_position and returns whether it applied
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_cursor_contract_e2e -- --nocapture
Projection cursor contract test passes against real Postgres + NATS

phase_17.title: Projection lag metrics and backlog gauges
phase_17.description: Emit operational metrics for projection freshness: projection_lag_ms, projection_backlog_count, projection_last_applied_age_seconds. Metrics must be derived from cursor timestamps and stream position. Expose via existing /metrics endpoints.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like an SRE for evented systems. Invariant: projection freshness is observable and comparable across modules. Failure mode to avoid: silent staleness where the system is 'up' but serving stale reads.
acceptance_criteria: projection_lag_ms and projection_backlog_count appear in /metrics
Metrics are labeled by projection_name and tenant/app context
A failing projection visibly increases lag/backlog
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_lag_metrics_e2e -- --nocapture
Metrics test passes; lag/backlog are emitted under controlled load

phase_17.title: Deterministic projection rebuild tool (blue/green swap)
phase_17.description: Create a rebuild tool that can: (1) build projections into a shadow schema/table set, (2) verify cursor and digest, (3) atomically swap readers to the rebuilt projection (blue/green). Must be deterministic given the same event stream and seed data.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a data-plane migration specialist. Invariant: a rebuild never serves partial state and swap is atomic. Failure mode to avoid: serving half-rebuilt projections or corrupting reads during rebuild.
acceptance_criteria: Rebuild tool supports shadow build + atomic swap
Swap is one transaction / one step from reader perspective
Rebuild emits a digest that is stable across two runs on same stream
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_rebuild_blue_green_e2e -- --nocapture
Blue/green rebuild E2E passes with real Postgres + NATS

phase_17.title: Fallback policy: HTTP fallback budgets + circuit breaker
phase_17.description: Implement explicit HTTP fallback behavior for reads when projections are stale/unavailable. Enforce a time budget (e.g., 200ms) and a circuit breaker to prevent cascading failure. Record fallback_invocation_count and fallback_latency_ms.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a resilience engineer. Invariant: fallback does not overload write services and is bounded by time/circuit rules. Failure mode to avoid: projection outage causing system-wide thundering herd to HTTP fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback only activates when projection is stale beyond threshold
Circuit breaker trips under sustained failures and prevents repeated fallbacks
Metrics emitted for fallback count and latency
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests projection_fallback_circuit_e2e -- --nocapture
Fallback E2E proves budget + breaker behavior with real services

phase_17.title: Replay certification: rebuild -> digest equality suite
phase_17.description: Add certification tests that rebuild at least two core projections (e.g., AR invoice summary, Payments attempt summary) twice from the same event stream and asserts identical digests, cursor end state, and row counts. Must run integrated against real Postgres + NATS.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a deterministic systems verifier. Invariant: replay produces the same read-model state, not just 'no crashes'. Failure mode to avoid: hidden nondeterminism in projection application order or time-derived fields.
acceptance_criteria: Two rebuild runs on identical stream produce identical digests
Cursor end positions match across runs
Digest computation is stable and versioned
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests replay_certification_digest_e2e -- --nocapture
Replay certification suite passes

phase_17.title: Audit spine: append-only audit log schema + writer API
phase_17.description: Create an append-only audit log (per module DB or centralized audit DB, consistent with your module boundaries). Define audit_event schema: audit_id, occurred_at, actor_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, before_hash/after_hash or before/after snapshots, causation_id/correlation_id/trace_id, mutation_class. Provide a single writer API used by modules.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a compliance auditor. Invariant: every audited mutation produces an immutable audit record with actor + causation metadata. Failure mode to avoid: mutable audit records or missing actor/correlation fields that make audits useless.
acceptance_criteria: Audit log table exists and is append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE allowed)
A shared audit writer API exists and is usable from all modules
Audit schema includes envelope metadata + mutation_class
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_append_only_e2e -- --nocapture
Append-only audit E2E passes and UPDATE/DELETE is rejected by DB

phase_17.title: Audit rules by mutation_class (policy enforcement)
phase_17.description: Implement enforceable rules: strict_immutable => no updates; compensating_required => must emit reversal/supersession linkage; mutable_with_audit => must emit field-level diff audit. Wire policy checks into mutation boundaries (guard/mutation layer).
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a financial controls engineer. Invariant: mutation_class determines required audit behavior and is enforced in code and DB. Failure mode to avoid: 'best effort' auditing where critical mutations slip through without audit artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Policy checks exist for strict_immutable, compensating_required, mutable_with_audit
Violations fail closed (mutation rejected)
Policy is unit-tested and exercised in E2E
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_policy_enforcement_e2e -- --nocapture
Audit policy enforcement E2E passes

phase_17.title: Field-level diff auditing for mutable_with_audit
phase_17.description: Implement field-diff capture for mutable_with_audit entities: record changed fields (name, old, new) or hashed snapshots with diff metadata. Ensure diffs are deterministic (stable ordering) and do not include nondeterministic timestamps unless explicitly allowed.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like an audit trail engineer. Invariant: diffs are complete, deterministic, and tied to actor/correlation metadata. Failure mode to avoid: partial diffs or nondeterministic ordering that breaks replay and verification.
acceptance_criteria: Diff entries are generated for at least one mutable_with_audit entity
Diff ordering is deterministic across runs
Audit entries include actor + trace/correlation ids
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_field_diff_e2e -- --nocapture
Field diff E2E passes and diffs match expected stable output

phase_17.title: Actor identity propagation into audit + envelope
phase_17.description: Standardize actor identity (service actor, user actor) propagation from HTTP entrypoints and internal schedulers into envelope metadata and audit entries. Define actor types and enforce presence where required by policy.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a security/compliance engineer. Invariant: every audited mutation has a non-empty actor identity with a defined type. Failure mode to avoid: anonymous mutations that cannot be attributed.
acceptance_criteria: Actor identity is present in audit entries for covered mutations
Schedulers use a service actor identity
E2E test validates actor fields are populated
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests audit_actor_propagation_e2e -- --nocapture
Actor propagation E2E passes

phase_17.title: Audit completeness oracle: mutation -> exactly one audit record
phase_17.description: Extend the existing oracle spine to include audit completeness checks for a defined set of mutations: for each mutation event (by idempotency_key/event_id), assert exactly one audit record exists and links via causation/correlation/trace.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a verification engineer. Invariant: audited mutations produce exactly one audit artifact (no gaps, no doubles). Failure mode to avoid: duplicate audit records on replay or missing records on edge paths.
acceptance_criteria: Oracle includes audit completeness checks for defined mutation set
E2E suite runs oracle after scenarios and validates audit completeness
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests -- --nocapture
All E2E tests pass with audit completeness checks enabled

phase_17.title: Tenant registry + provisioning schema baseline
phase_17.description: Create a control-plane tenant registry (lightweight) that tracks tenant_id, status, created_at, module schema versions, and environment. Provide a deterministic provisioning sequence definition (steps and checks).
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a platform operations engineer. Invariant: tenant provisioning is repeatable and observable with a single source of truth. Failure mode to avoid: ad-hoc tenant setup that drifts and breaks upgrades.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant registry schema exists and can store per-module schema versions
Provisioning steps are defined and include verification checks
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests tenant_registry_smoke_e2e -- --nocapture
Tenant registry smoke E2E passes

phase_17.title: Tenant provisioning CLI (create/activate/verify)
phase_17.description: Build a CLI tool to provision tenants deterministically: create tenant record, initialize required module DBs, run migrations, seed minimal data, and run verification checks (health/version endpoints).
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a release engineer. Invariant: tenant creation produces a fully-migrated, verifiable tenant with no manual steps. Failure mode to avoid: partial provisioning that passes superficially but fails during billing cycles.
acceptance_criteria: CLI supports: tenant create, tenant activate, tenant verify
Verify calls real /ready and /version endpoints for all modules
Provisioning produces deterministic outcomes given same inputs
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo run -p tenantctl -- tenant create --tenant t1 && cargo run -p tenantctl -- tenant verify --tenant t1
CLI completes successfully and verification passes

phase_17.title: Multi-tenant migration runner (N tenants, deterministic)
phase_17.description: Implement a migration runner that can apply migrations across N tenant databases deterministically with bounded concurrency, clear logging, and failure reporting. Must not require tenant_id threaded through app code; this is an ops tool.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a database fleet operator. Invariant: migrations across many tenant DBs are repeatable, ordered, and safe under concurrency. Failure mode to avoid: partial fleet migration with no visibility or inconsistent schema versions.
acceptance_criteria: Runner can migrate N tenant DBs with bounded parallelism
Runner records per-tenant schema version results into tenant registry
Failures are resumable without reapplying completed tenants
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo run -p tenantctl -- fleet migrate --tenants 10 --parallel 4
Fleet migration completes; registry reflects schema versions

phase_17.title: Tenant suspension/deprovision flow (safe + auditable)
phase_17.description: Implement tenant lifecycle transitions: suspend (stop schedulers / block writes per policy), and deprovision (archive/retain per retention policy). Must emit audit records and update registry.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a compliance + ops engineer. Invariant: tenant lifecycle changes are reversible (where allowed) and auditable, and do not leak resources. Failure mode to avoid: suspended tenants still mutating ledgers or deprovision deleting data that must be retained.
acceptance_criteria: Suspend blocks billing schedulers and write mutations per policy
Deprovision follows retention policy and records actions in audit log
Registry reflects lifecycle status accurately
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests tenant_lifecycle_suspend_deprovision_e2e -- --nocapture
Lifecycle E2E passes

phase_17.title: Scale simulation: 100 tenants billing digest stability
phase_17.description: Extend deterministic simulation harness patterns to run 100 tenants through compressed cycles, then validate projection lag bounds, audit completeness, and digest stability. This is not performance tuning; it is correctness-under-scale validation.
phase_17.how_to_think: Think like a systems verification lead. Invariant: correctness properties (no duplicates, audit completeness, projection determinism) hold as tenant count scales. Failure mode to avoid: scale introduces race conditions and nondeterminism.
acceptance_criteria: 100-tenant run completes with stable digests for key projections
Audit completeness oracle passes for covered mutations
Projection lag metrics remain within configured bounds during run
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests scale_100_tenants_truth_at_scale_e2e -- --nocapture
Scale E2E passes

phase_18.title: Release pipeline: promote artifacts across environments
phase_18.description: Create a promotion pipeline that builds versioned module artifacts, publishes them to an internal registry (or GitHub releases if that is your baseline), and supports promotion dev -> staging -> prod with immutable artifact hashes. Must tie module version, schema version, and projection/audit versions together.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like a release manager. Invariant: the exact artifact deployed is traceable and immutable across environments. Failure mode to avoid: environment drift and 'works in staging' due to rebuilding different binaries.
acceptance_criteria: CI produces immutable artifacts with checksums
Promotion uses existing artifacts (no rebuild) and records provenance
Deploy metadata includes module version + schema version + projection version
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests release_provenance_smoke_e2e -- --nocapture
Release provenance E2E passes

phase_18.title: Secrets and config hardening (env contract + validation)
phase_18.description: Define a strict configuration contract per module (required env vars, defaults, and validation). Add startup validation that fails fast with clear errors. Ensure tenant tools also validate required config.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like an operations hardening engineer. Invariant: services never start with partial/misconfigured secrets or unsafe defaults. Failure mode to avoid: running with missing keys leading to corrupt external integrations or insecure behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Each module validates required env vars on startup
Validation errors are explicit and actionable
E2E test proves a module refuses to start with missing required config
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests config_validation_failfast_e2e -- --nocapture
Config failfast E2E passes

phase_18.title: Service-to-service auth (signed internal requests)
phase_18.description: Introduce service-to-service authentication for internal HTTP fallbacks and ops endpoints (where applicable). Use signed tokens or mTLS (pick one consistent pattern). Ensure tenant tools authenticate when calling /ready, /version, fallback routes.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like a platform security engineer. Invariant: internal endpoints are not anonymously callable and requests are verifiable. Failure mode to avoid: unauthorized internal calls triggering mutations or leaking sensitive status data.
acceptance_criteria: Internal HTTP calls include verifiable auth
Unauthorized requests are rejected with 401/403
E2E test confirms authenticated success and unauthenticated failure
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests service_to_service_auth_e2e -- --nocapture
Service-to-service auth E2E passes

phase_18.title: RBAC for operator actions (tenant lifecycle + rebuild)
phase_18.description: Add role-based authorization for operator actions: tenant suspend/deprovision, projection rebuild/swap, fleet migrate. Define roles (operator, admin, auditor) and enforce them in tools and any HTTP endpoints used.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like a governance/security lead. Invariant: high-impact operational actions require explicit authorization. Failure mode to avoid: anyone with network access can suspend tenants or rebuild projections.
acceptance_criteria: Roles are defined and enforced for operational actions
Unauthorized role attempts are rejected and audited
E2E test covers allowed/denied cases
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests rbac_operator_actions_e2e -- --nocapture
RBAC E2E passes

phase_18.title: Dashboards: SLO views for lag, UNKNOWN, audit failures
phase_18.description: Create Grafana dashboards (or your chosen stack) for: projection lag/backlog, payment UNKNOWN aging, invariant failures, audit completeness errors, tenant provisioning status. Dashboards must match metrics produced in Phase 17/16.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like an SRE building operational truth. Invariant: operators can see leading indicators before customers call. Failure mode to avoid: having metrics but no actionable visualization and therefore no operational feedback loop.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard JSON/provisioning exists in repo
Panels map to real metrics names and labels
A smoke test (manual ok) confirms dashboards load with live data
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d
Grafana loads dashboards and panels show non-empty data under test load

phase_18.title: DR drill: restore + replay rebuild + oracle pass
phase_18.description: Automate a disaster recovery drill that: restores DB backups into scratch, replays events to rebuild projections, runs cross-module oracle + audit completeness checks, and outputs a digest report. This is a full-stack correctness drill.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like a disaster recovery engineer. Invariant: you can restore and re-derive read models and still pass oracles. Failure mode to avoid: backups restore but system cannot reach correct state due to missing events or nondeterministic projections.
acceptance_criteria: DR drill script exists and is runnable end-to-end
Oracle passes after restore + rebuild
Produces a digest report artifact
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && bash tools/backup/dr_drill_restore_rebuild.sh
Script completes and reports ORACLE PASS + stable digests

phase_18.title: Rate limiting and overload protection for read paths
phase_18.description: Add rate limits for read-heavy endpoints and fallback paths, with tenant-aware quotas. Ensure overload does not cascade into write services. Emit reject metrics.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like a production traffic engineer. Invariant: overload is contained and does not degrade write correctness. Failure mode to avoid: read spikes causing fallback storms and starving core billing mutations.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant-aware rate limit exists for selected endpoints
Fallback path has tighter limits than normal reads
E2E test shows limits engage and system remains healthy
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests rate_limit_overload_protection_e2e -- --nocapture
Rate limiting E2E passes

phase_18.title: Compliance export: audit + ledger extract per tenant
phase_18.description: Provide a deterministic export tool to produce tenant-scoped extracts for audit logs and core ledgers (AR/Payments/GL) with stable ordering and checksums. Output format: CSV/JSON Lines with manifest and hashes.
phase_18.how_to_think: Think like an external auditor integrator. Invariant: exports are tenant-scoped, complete, and tamper-evident. Failure mode to avoid: partial exports or nondeterministic ordering that prevents reconciliation.
acceptance_criteria: Export tool generates manifests with checksums
Exports are stable across two runs with same source state
Includes audit log + at least AR invoices + payment attempts + GL entries
verification_commands: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.infrastructure.yml up -d && cargo test -p e2e-tests compliance_export_determinism_e2e -- --nocapture
Compliance export E2E passes
